query: What is the dominant inward force in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk, and what is the dominant outward force?

row.paper: Liu, Z.Y., et al., 2024. Juno observations of Jupiter's magnetodisk plasma: Implications for equilibrium and dynamics. JGR Space Physics, 129(11), e2024JA032976. DOI:10.1029/2024JA032976
row.query: What is the dominant inward force in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk, and what is the dominant outward force?

tool_input.query: Jupiter magnetodisk current sheet force balance

tool_input.summary:
Physics answer: In Jupiter’s dawnside magnetodisk the dominant **inward** (planetward) force is typically the **magnetic curvature/tension (J×B tension) force** from the stretched/bent magnetic field lines. The dominant **outward** (radially away from Jupiter) force is typically the **centrifugal/inertial force** of the rotating (partly corotating) iogenic plasma, with the plasma pressure-gradient force also contributing outward but usually secondary in the dawn sector.

Relevant PDS datasets (useful to quantify these forces via B-field + plasma moments + trajectory; no PDS3 equivalents were returned by catalog search):

candidates.title: Galileo Orbiter at Jupiter Magnetometer High Resolution Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides calibrated Jupiter magnetic-field vectors needed to estimate magnetic tension/curvature and magnetic pressure forces in the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: Galileo Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides plasma moments/densities/flows to estimate centrifugal (ρvφ²/r) and pressure-gradient contributions in the dawnside magnetodisk.

candidates.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides spacecraft trajectory/geometry to bin observations by local time (dawnside) and radial distance when doing force-balance calculations.

candidates.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle pressures can be incorporated into total plasma pressure used for outward pressure-gradient estimates in the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: Voyager 1 Magnetometer Jupiter Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Independent magnetic-field measurements in Jupiter’s magnetosphere for comparative magnetodisk structure/force-balance studies.

candidates.title: Voyager 1 Plasma Science Experiment (PLS) Jupiter Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Plasma parameters from Voyager for estimating centrifugal and pressure forces during the Jupiter flyby interval.

candidates.title: Voyager 2 Plasma Science Experiment (PLS) Jupiter Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Additional plasma constraints for outward-force terms from a second flyby/local-time sampling.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
What is the dominant inward force in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk, and what is the dominant outward force?

parts.args: {"summary":"Physics answer: In Jupiter’s dawnside magnetodisk the dominant **inward** (planetward) force is typically the **magnetic curvature/tension (J×B tension) force** from the stretched/bent magnetic field lines. The dominant **outward** (radially away from Jupiter) force is typically the **centrifugal/inertial force** of the rotating (partly corotating) iogenic plasma, with the plasma pressure-gradient force also contributing outward but usually secondary in the dawn sector.\n\nRelevant PDS datasets (useful to quantify these forces via B-field + plasma moments + trajectory; no PDS3 equivalents were returned by catalog search):","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-mag-jup-calibrated::1.1","title":"Galileo Orbiter at Jupiter Magnetometer High Resolution Calibrated Bundle","mission":"Galileo","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides calibrated Jupiter magnetic-field vectors needed to estimate magnetic tension/curvature and magnetic pressure forces in the magnetodisk."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-pls-jup-cal::1.0","title":"Galileo Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Calibrated Bundle","mission":"Galileo","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides plasma moments/densities/flows to estimate centrifugal (ρvφ²/r) and pressure-gradient contributions in the dawnside magnetodisk."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-traj-jup::1.0","title":"Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Bundle","mission":"Galileo","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides spacecraft trajectory/geometry to bin observations by local time (dawnside) and radial distance when doing force-balance calculations."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-epd-cal-corrected::1.0","title":"Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Data Bundle","mission":"Galileo","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Energetic particle pressures can be incorporated into total plasma pressure used for outward pressure-gradient estimates in the magnetodisk."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:vg1-mag-jup::1.0","title":"Voyager 1 Magnetometer Jupiter Data Bundle","mission":"Voyager","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Independent magnetic-field measurements in Jupiter’s magnetosphere for comparative magnetodisk structure/force-balance studies."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:vg1-pls-jup::1.0","title":"Voyager 1 Plasma Science Experiment (PLS) Jupiter Data Bundle","mission":"Voyager","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Plasma parameters from Voyager for estimating centrifugal and pressure forces during the Jupiter flyby interval."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:vg2-pls-jup::1.1","title":"Voyager 2 Plasma Science Experiment (PLS) Jupiter Data Bundle","mission":"Voyager","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Additional plasma constraints for outward-force terms from a second flyby/local-time sampling."}]}
